rulings.Case Title: MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO VS. D.N. SECURITY SERVICES, LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearong And Application For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 27, 2009, line 2, PLAINTIFF MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO'S Notice Of Hearing And Application For Attorney'S Fees And Costs. Application for attorney's fees and costs granted. Plaintiff is the prevailing party entitled to attorney's fees under Labor Code section 218.5, 226(e), 2699(g)(1) and 1194, and Code of Civil Procedure section 1021.5. The hourly rates charged are reasonable, and the hours spent reasonable and necessary. Attorney's fees granted in the Lodestar amount of $126,744 plus costs in the amount of $3,749.92. =(302CWW/DC)

rulings.Case Title: JIAN XIN WANG et al VS. EAST BAY LLC et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 27, 2009, line 9, Plaintiff Bank of America National Bank's Order to Show Cause why apointment of Receiver should not be confirmed, and why Preliminary Injunction should not be granted. Motion Granted. =(302CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: COCOA DEVELOPMENT RETAIL LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED VS. MARCHE RUE LLC DBA MARCHE A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Cocoa Development Retail, Llc'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 27, 2009, line 10, Plaintiff Cocoa Development Retail, LLC'S Order to Show Cause regarding Preliminary Injunction. Motion for preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff fails to show a likelihood of irreparable harm. Insufficient showing that there is a reasonable probability that plaintiff will prevail on the merits at trial. A mandatory injunction pending trial is not permitted except in extreme caes where the right is clearly established. See Teachers Ins. & Annuity Assn. vs. Furlotti (1999) 70 Cal.App. 4th 1487, 1493. =(302CWW/JW)
